Is cabozantinib effective for hepatocellular carcinoma?

While cabozantinib acts via kinase inhibition—a mechanism applicable theoretically to many cancers—the evidence shows **only confirmed use in AML**, not in hepatocellular carcinoma.
No study, trial, or indication referencing hepatocellular carcinoma appears anywhere in the text corpus.

The evidence supports cabozantinib's use exclusively in hematologic malignancies such as acute myeloid leukemia. There is no mention of its use or efficacy in hepatocellular carcinoma.
